title: Fecal sample collection from mice
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6gqbv","text":"Fecal sample collection from mice to use for SCFA or cytokines measurement.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"eq5tl","text":"- 1.6mL eppendorf tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"51ag5","text":"- Clean and empty cages (one per animal)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8nck1","text":"- Dry ice","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cctm6","text":"- A precision scale","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"534rf","text":"- Forceps","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fquu8","text":"- A small beaker","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fmdd9","text":"- Ethanol ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aridq","text":"- Kimwipes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare one clean and empty cage per animal (without food, water or bedding). Label each cage with the tag of the animal to avoid confusion later on.
Label dry eppendorf 1.6 mL tubes. Weight them with a precision scale. Place the tubes on dry ice.
Prepare a beaker with ethanol and kimwipes to clean forceps between each collection to avoid contamination between samples.
Place mice in their respective cages.

Collect all fecal pellets produced with forceps. Tranfer them directly in the tube on dry ice. Try to harvest them quickly as soon as they appear to prevent them from drying out. Do not collect pellets that were in urine or dried up pellets. The collection can be performed within a set time or to reach a sufficient number of pellets for subsequent studies. Clean and dry the forceps between each collection.
Weight the tube with the pellets and calculate the weight of the pellet for subsequent normalization. Be careful to minimize humidity due to dry ice.
Quickly store tubes into -80 °C until analysis.
creator: Amandine Even
